title: On sharp anisotropic Hardy inequalities
summary: Recently, Yanyan Li and Xukai Yan showed the following interesting Hardy inequalities with anisotropic weights: Let $n\geq 2$, $p \geq 1$, $pα> 1-n$, $p(α+ β)> -n$, then there exists $C > 0$ such that $$\||x|^β|x'|^{α+1} \nabla u\|_{L^p(\mathbb{R}^n)} \geq C\||x|^β|x'|^αu\|_{L^p(\mathbb{R}^n)}, \quad \forall\; u\in C_c^1(\mathbb{R}^n).$$ Here $x' = (x_1,\ldots, x_{n-1}, 0)$ for $x = (x_i) \in \mathbb{R}^n$. In this note, we will determine the best constant for the above estimate when $p=2$ or $β\geq 0$. Moreover, as refinement for very special case of Li-Yan's result in Adv. Math. 2023, we provide explicit estimate for the anisotropic $L^p$-Caffarelli-Kohn-Nirenberg inequality.
